verdict: AI displacement risk: moderate, with potential for significant disruption in the next 5 years.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Paramedics perform tasks that are increasingly automated, such as taking vital signs and administering medications.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: While paramedics have some autonomy in their decision-making, the scope of practice varies between countries, and AI-powered tools may eventually standardize procedures.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Provides authoritative information on the tasks performed by paramedics

evidence_ledger.meta: Summary of Paramedic roles and responsibilities
evidence_ledger.contribution: Offers a general overview of the paramedic profession

adjacent_careers.name: Emergency Medical Technician (EMT)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: EMTs and paramedics often work together, and EMTs may eventually adopt more advanced training to become paramedics.

adjacent_careers.name: Nurse
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Nurses and paramedics share some similarities in their roles, but nurses typically require more education and training.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered diagnostic tool approved for use in ambulances
recent_developments.impact: This tool may automate some tasks currently performed by paramedics, potentially disrupting the profession.

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
